steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage states that Reichert’s team found corals may store up to 20 million kilograms of microplastics annually in their skeletons and tissues. This specific claim about coral actively storing large quantities of microplastics is the main idea.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice D highlights ecologists predicting corals store large amounts of microplastics, mirroring the study’s central finding.

  • A focuses on reef-building (not mentioned in the study)
  • B introduces vague questions (which weren’t addressed in the text)
  • C only defines microplastics
.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
